frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment:
Ramesh: Arre bhai, maa ke haath ki roti aur saag ki baat hi alag thi, poori zindagi ghar-parivaar ke liye chulha sambhala, kabhi thaki bhi nahi boli. Aajkal ki bahuen sab cheez jaldi-jaldi chahti hain, par ghar ka swaad aur barkat tabhi rehti hai jab aurat dil se rasoi sambhale.
Brother: Haan, woh baat to sahi hai, maa jaisi seva sabke bas ki nahi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it sentimentalizes a mother's unpaid domestic labor as her natural expression of love and sets it up as the standard for daughters-in-law. It encodes glorification of sacrifice, labor erasure, and comparison of older mothers with 'aajkal ki bahuen'.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 52. A farmer in rural UP. OBC Hindu. Your 17 year old daughter Rekha has topped her class 12 board exams and her teacher has come to your house personally to tell you she should apply to colleges in Lucknow. You have listened to the teacher politely. The teacher has left. You are now sitting with your brother in the courtyard. You are telling your brother what the teacher said and what you have decided and why you think sending Rekha to a college 90 kilometres away is not something you are going to do and what you think education beyond a point does to girls from families like yours.
